channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: As the Trump administration targets funding, scientists look beyond the U.S. to work
item.description: A growing number of prominent U.S. scientists are taking jobs in other countries, including the U.K. They say Trump's impact on U.S. science funding is a factor.
item.content:encoded: A growing number of prominent U.S. scientists are taking jobs in other countries, including the U.K. They say Trump's impact on U.S. science funding is a factor.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Tips for taking care of your pets when fireworks are booming
item.description: Communities around the U.S. are putting on epic fireworks shows for America's 250th birthday — but that spells trouble for dogs and cats. Here's a guide to help the animals in your life.
item.content:encoded:
Communities around the U.S. are putting on epic fireworks shows for America's 250th birthday — but that spells trouble for dogs and cats. Here's a guide to help the animals in your life.

(Image credit: Joseph Prezioso)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Critical fire weather complicates firefighting efforts in massive Utah wildfire
item.description: Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.
item.content:encoded:
Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.

(Image credit: Ty ONeil)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Australian Prof. Ajay Narendra discusses his team's discovery of the 'ballista spider'
item.description: Prof. Ajay Narendra from Macquarie University in Australia tells NPR about his team's discovery of a particularly rare, and high-powered, hunting method of the informally named "ballista spider."
item.content:encoded: Prof. Ajay Narendra from Macquarie University in Australia tells NPR about his team's discovery of a particularly rare, and high-powered, hunting method of the informally named "ballista spider."
item.dc:creator: Ryan Benk

item.title: What made the deadly Venezuelan earthquakes different
item.description: It appears the two big earthquakes in Venezuela that occurred in rapid succession may have involved two separate fault lines. Several faults intersect in this tectonically complex region.
item.content:encoded:
It appears the two big earthquakes in Venezuela that occurred in rapid succession may have involved two separate fault lines. Several faults intersect in this tectonically complex region.

(Image credit: Edilzon Gamez)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: This week in science: Exoplanetary salt clouds, ancient families and laughter's origin
item.description: Hosts from NPR's science podcast Short Wave talk about an exoplanet with pink, salty clouds, the surprising social structure of ancient human relatives and the origins of laughter.
item.content:encoded: Hosts from NPR's science podcast Short Wave talk about an exoplanet with pink, salty clouds, the surprising social structure of ancient human relatives and the origins of laughter.
item.dc:creator: Regina G. Barber

item.title: A volunteer otter watcher helped make a scientific discovery
item.description: A man who volunteered as an otter spotter made some scientific discoveries along the way.
item.content:encoded: A man who volunteered as an otter spotter made some scientific discoveries along the way.
item.dc:creator: Elena Neale-Sacks

item.title: Leading Lebanese conservationist dies after an Israeli airstrike on her home
item.description: Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.
item.content:encoded:
Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.

(Image credit: Jihad Seqlawi)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Butterflies may hold clues to longer lives
item.description: Why do some butterflies live for months while others survive only weeks? Tufts University researcher Jessica Foley explains what Heliconius butterflies can teach us about aging.
item.content:encoded: Why do some butterflies live for months while others survive only weeks? Tufts University researcher Jessica Foley explains what Heliconius butterflies can teach us about aging.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: A new app could help streamline loon data by enabling citizens to log observations
item.description: Loons, beloved American waterbirds, face threats from climate change and pollution. An oil spill settlement funds a new phone app helping non-scientists to aid research on these birds.
item.content:encoded: Loons, beloved American waterbirds, face threats from climate change and pollution. An oil spill settlement funds a new phone app helping non-scientists to aid research on these birds.
item.dc:creator: Abagael Giles
